participants: Hana Wells (Sales Ops Manager, Acme Corp)
transcript:
[00:03] Sarah Johnson: Thanks for having Hana join today — I wanted to make sure we covered the operational side too.
[00:14] Ben Martinez: Of course. She's the one who actually lives in the tool every day.
[00:21] Hana Wells: It's become essential, honestly. I don't know how we managed pipeline without it.
[00:32] Sarah Johnson: That's great to hear. I was looking at your usage numbers before this call — API calls are up 40% month-over-month. What's driving that?
[00:45] Ben Martinez: We automated three workflows that were manual before. The team took to it faster than I expected. And honestly, I've been thinking about what else we could do with it.
[01:00] Sarah Johnson: Tell me more.
[01:05] Ben Martinez: We're planning to roll out to the sales team in Q3. Right now it's just Sales Ops and a couple of the RevOps folks. But we've got 40 salespeople who could benefit from the forecasting module.
[01:22] Sarah Johnson: Interesting. What's the use case for the reps specifically?
[01:30] Ben Martinez: Pipeline hygiene. Our reps are notoriously bad at updating Salesforce. If we can surface the signals automatically, that's a win for the whole team.
[01:44] Hana Wells: We're at 95 seats right now. If we add the sales team that's 40 more people. We'd be well over our current limit.
[01:55] Sarah Johnson: Right — you're currently on Business 100. If you added 40 seats, we'd need to look at what that looks like.
[02:08] Ben Martinez: What does it look like? I don't know what the pricing is for adding seats mid-term.
[02:18] Sarah Johnson: I can put together a custom quote. The short answer is we'd move you to a Business 150 plan — it's tiered so the per-seat cost actually goes down as you add more.
[02:33] Ben Martinez: That's good to know. Is there flexibility on timing? Our budget cycle resets in Q3 and Chloe — our CFO — is the one who needs to approve anything over $25K.
[02:48] Sarah Johnson: Totally understand. We can time this to align with Q3 budget. I'd actually suggest doing a separate 30-minute call specifically on the expansion — just you, me, and Chloe if she's open to it.
[03:05] Ben Martinez: She would want to see an ROI case before that. She's data-driven.
[03:15] Sarah Johnson: I can build that. Time savings, adoption rate, the automation wins you mentioned. I'll put something together before we loop her in.
[03:28] Hana Wells: That would help a lot. I can give you the numbers on time saved — I've been tracking it informally since January.
[03:38] Sarah Johnson: Perfect. Let's do this: I'll have a proposal and ROI one-pager ready in two weeks, and we'll schedule the expansion call for late April once you've had a chance to review it with Chloe.
[03:52] Ben Martinez: That works. One thing I want to make sure — we've got a minor integration issue with our CRM that's been open for a week. Can we get that closed before we do anything else?
[04:05] Sarah Johnson: Absolutely. I'll escalate that today and get you a resolution date by end of week.
[04:15] Ben Martinez: Great. This has been a good conversation. I'll be honest — when we signed up I wasn't sure we'd get this much value out of it. It's transformed how our RevOps team works.
[04:30] Sarah Johnson: That's exactly what we're here for. I'll have everything ready in two weeks.
